description_long: This object is a vintage-style gramophone record player featuring a large, flared brass horn mounted on a dark wooden base. The horn is polygonal and golden, with a wide opening that narrows into a curved neck, attached to the right side of the base. The base is rectangular, with beveled edges and decorative gold accents at the corners, and a visible label plate on the front. On top of the base sits a black turntable platter with a vinyl record, and a gold tonearm extending over the record. The tonearm is ornate, with a sculpted design and a small stylus at its end. The overall appearance is classic and decorative, evoking early 20th-century audio equipment.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large golden horn, dark wooden base, and ornate gold tonearm over a black turntable platter.
description_view_0: The front-left view highlights the wide opening of the golden horn, the decorative base, and the visible record on the platter.
description_view_1: The left side view shows the full length of the horn and tonearm, with the record and platter clearly visible on the base.
description_view_2: The rear view centers on the back of the horn and the base, with the tonearm and platter partially obscured.
description_view_3: The right side view displays the horn's curvature, the ornate tonearm, and the record on the platter, with the base's details visible.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and black, occupying the central top area of the base. It appears to be made of plastic or metal, with a vinyl record placed on top. The platter is smooth, with a subtle sheen, and the record has a visible label in the center.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, ornate, and gold-colored, extending from the right side of the base over the platter. It is medium in length, slightly curved, and features decorative sculpting. The tonearm is positioned over the record, suggesting it is ready to play.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is visible as a small, pointed component at the end of the tonearm, appearing metallic and distinct from the rest of the arm. It is attached and positioned over the record.
